title: Lesson 20 — The Charmer

words.definition: friendly, good-natured, and easy to talk to
words.example: An affable host who made every guest feel at home.

words.definition: having a friendly and pleasant manner
words.example: An amiable colleague, always ready to lend a hand.

words.definition: pleasant because of shared interests or a similar disposition
words.example: A congenial group of like-minded travelers.
A cordial welcome greeted them at the door.
The genial innkeeper knew every guest by name.

words.definition: suave, courteous, and refined in manner
words.example: An urbane diplomat at ease in any room.

words.definition: charming, confident, and elegant, especially in manner
words.example: A suave negotiator who never lost his composure.
A debonair guest arrived in a crisp white dinner jacket.
A gracious loser, she was first to congratulate the winner.

words.definition: generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or someone less powerful
words.example: Magnanimous in victory, she praised her defeated opponent.

words.definition: familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures
words.example: A cosmopolitan city where a hundred languages mingle.

words.definition: willing to talk and engage with others; friendly
words.example: A sociable newcomer who joined every club in a week.

story: Everyone angled for an invitation to Devlin's parties, because Devlin himself was so [[affable|affable]] and [[amiable|amiable]]. He gathered a [[congenial|congenial]] crowd, offered a [[cordial|cordial]] welcome at the door, and drifted among them, [[genial|genial]] and unhurried. He was [[urbane|urbane]], [[suave|suave]], and frankly [[debonair|debonair]], yet never cold — always [[gracious|gracious]] to the shyest guest and [[magnanimous|magnanimous]] toward rivals he might easily have snubbed. Well-traveled and [[cosmopolitan|cosmopolitan]], he could draw out even the least [[sociable|sociable]] visitor before the night was through.
